subject: Databases
title: Build a Blameless Culture
description: A quick win: understand Build a Blameless Culture.

hook.text: An engineer drops a production table. Do you fire them? Or ask why the system allowed it?

buildup.text: Blameless post-mortems focus on the system, not the person. Why was it possible? What guardrails were missing?

discovery.text: GitLab's live-streamed recovery built trust. They showed the problem, the process, and the fix. No finger-pointing.

twist.text: Blame culture makes people hide mistakes. Hidden mistakes become bigger incidents. Openness enables faster recovery.

climax.text: Write post-mortems that anyone can read. Share them across teams. The best learning comes from real failures.

punchline.text: Blame the system, not the person. Fix the process. Share the learning.

quiz.question: Why is a blameless culture important for preventing future database incidents?
options: It encourages people to report mistakes early instead of hiding them, enabling faster fixes
It eliminates all human errors permanently
Blameless culture means no one is responsible for anything
